title: Wind, slopes and Bangalore afternoons
microFact: Into the wind, club up and swing EASIER — hard swings add spin, and spin makes headwinds crueler.
lesson:
Adjustments that separate course players from range players:

WIND: a headwind punishes SPIN — a hard-swung ball balloons and falls short. The counterintuitive fix: take MORE club and swing SMOOTHER ("when it's breezy, swing easy"). Rough guide: one extra club per 10 km/h into the wind; downwind helps less than headwind hurts. Crosswind? Aim off and let it ride. Check wind at the GREEN too — treetops and the flag itself.

Your 1–5 PM Bangalore slot is the windiest, warmest slice of the day — afternoon thermals are real, especially Feb–May. The ball also flies slightly farther in hot air. Learn your afternoon distances as your TRUE distances.

SLOPES: uphill shot = plays longer (add a club); downhill = shorter. Ball ABOVE your feet wants to go left; BELOW your feet, right — aim to allow for it.

LIES: from rough, expect less spin and more roll-out ("flyer"). Off hardpan, ball first, low expectations.

None of this needs talent — just the habit of asking "what changes this shot today?" before pulling a club. Your caddie knows all of it; ask him and cross-check.

quiz.question: Playing into a stiff headwind, the smart adjustment is…
options: Swing harder with the same club
Take more club and swing smoother
Aim higher to ride over the wind
Use less club — wind lifts the ball
quiz.explanation: Hard swings add backspin, and headwinds feast on spin. More club + smooth tempo keeps the flight controlled and penetrating.
